user_message: Can you extract the territory planning from this meeting transcript for Iron Creek Mining? I met with them last week but can't remember the details.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-23 08:42 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Iron Creek Mining territory planning

There's been a change in our timeline for territory planning. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
Date: 2024-02-10 08:36 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Iron Creek Mining territory planning

Just checking in on the territory planning proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-01 08:18 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Iron Creek Mining territory planning

Our team encountered some challenges with territory planning. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Iron Creek Mining: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-L trap (Stakeholder-map refresh as separate proposal vs inline verification); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-L failure mode (Stakeholder-map refresh as separate proposal vs inline verification)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-L -- Stakeholder-map refresh as separate proposal vs inline verification
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
